core_payload.content:
# Calendar

## 1. Role
Calendar is the intelligent temporal surface of N1Hub where commitments, plans, reminders, review windows, and external obligations are projected into lived time without flattening their scheduling meaning.

## 2. Temporal Contract
Calendar distinguishes fixed commitments, provisional plans, review blocks, focus windows, recovery space, and background autonomy windows. Time therefore expresses not only when something is scheduled, but what kind of scheduling truth it represents.

## 3. Operational Consequence
The user can see whether a day is carrying committed execution, exploratory planning, overloaded aspiration, or quiet recovery. Calendar acts as a rhythm-aware control layer rather than a passive grid of dates.

## 4. Philosophical Alignment
Calendar supports To Dig Deep by making lived time an intelligent part of the second brain instead of a flat scheduling surface.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Calendar defines the temporal surface that organizes commitments, provisional plans, reminders, review windows, focus blocks, recovery space, and autonomy windows inside N1Hub. It distinguishes different kinds of scheduling truth so time is not treated as a flat list of dates and obligations. The capsule matters because operators need to see whether a day carries committed execution, exploratory planning, overload risk, or deliberate recovery. It turns calendar state into an interpretable planning layer that supports rhythm, review, and disciplined control over lived time.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Removed meta-language so the capsule states present temporal doctrine directly.
